name: glamour (Markdown Rendering in Terminals)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Charmbracelet (Maas Lalani, Christian Rocha)

performance.notes: Markdown parsing and ANSI rendering; minimal overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Render Markdown as beautifully styled terminal output. glamour.Render(input, 'dark') or glamour.Render(input, 'light'). Custom styles via StyleConfig. Styles: dark, light, notty, dracula, tokyo-night, pink. Used in Charm's Soft Serve, Glow, and many CLI tools. Returns string with ANSI escape codes. golang.org/x/term for terminal width detection. Part of Charmbracelet ecosystem. Pair with lipgloss for custom styling. Glow is the standalone glamour-powered Markdown viewer CLI.

gotchas: ANSI output may not render in all terminals/CI environments
Custom styles require learning StyleConfig struct
Width detection needs terminal size query
best_when: CLI tools displaying formatted documentation or help text
TUI apps needing rich text rendering (README, changelogs, docs)
Go CLIs integrating with AI outputs that return Markdown
avoid_when: Non-terminal output (use goldmark for HTML rendering)
summary: glamour renders Markdown as beautifully styled terminal output using ANSI escape codes. glamour.Render(input, 'dark') with themes: dark, light, dracula, tokyo-night. Used in Charm's Glow, Soft Serve, and many Go CLIs. Part of Charmbracelet ecosystem. Best for Go CLI tools that need to display rich Markdown content — particularly useful for AI-generated content, documentation, and changelogs in terminal interfaces.
